nda.title: Non-Disclosure Agreement
nda.subtitle: Confidentiality & Access Terms
body: **Confidentiality**: All information contained in this dashboard is strictly confidential and proprietary to RAOSS HK COMPANY LIMITED.
**Purpose**: Access is granted solely for the purpose of contributing to the QR114i Salaam Stream Hub project.
**No Distribution**: You may not copy, screenshot, download, forward, or share any document, specification, or data contained herein.
**Return / Destruction**: Upon project completion or request, you agree to return or permanently delete all project materials.
**Breach**: Any breach may result in immediate revocation of access, legal action, and claims for damages.
**Acknowledgement**: By clicking "I Agree", you confirm that you have read, understood, and accept all terms above.
nda.checkbox: I have read and agree to the Non-Disclosure Agreement.
nda.alert_decline: You must agree to the NDA to access this dashboard.

executive_summary.title: Executive Summary & Core Architecture
executive_summary.intro: This project is built around the JeiLi AC792N (梧桐二代) Multimedia SoC — a unified solution that handles audio playback, display driving, WiFi/BLE, and power management in a single QFN package.

items.title: Requirements & Datasheet Review
items.desc: AC792N capabilities evaluated. Hardware design guide reviewed. Vendor samples secured.

items.title: SDK Environment Setup
items.desc: JeiLi IDE and JL_SDK configured. LVGL porting started.

items.title: PCB Layout & Prototype Build (EVT)
items.desc: 4-layer PCB with impedance control. First prototype assembly.

items.title: Firmware Integration & BLE Provisioning
items.desc: FreeRTOS tasks: display driver, audio MP3 decoder, WiFi STA, BLE GATT server.

items.title: Design Validation Test (DVT)
items.desc: Full feature validation. Co-existence interference testing.

items.title: PVT & Certification
items.desc: CE/FCC/IC certification. Qi wireless charging certification. 100-unit pilot run.

items.title: Mass Production Ramp
items.desc: Tooling finalized. 5K units/month target. OTA infrastructure live.

risks.title: Risks & Mitigation
rows: Use 16-bit RGB565 if 24-bit bandwidth is insufficient at 40MHz pixel clock.
Separate analog ground. Follow datasheet Section 4.5 & 4.6 layout rules.
Plastic RF window in top 20mm of device shell — mandatory.
Use ±10ppm 24MHz and ±10ppm 32.768kHz crystals from approved vendors.

responsibility.title: Team Responsibility Matrix
rows: Mobile app (iOS/Android), BLE provisioning, prayer time API, OTA hosting
AC792N schematic, 4-layer PCB layout, power tree, audio routing
FreeRTOS app, LVGL UI, MP3 playback, WiFi/BLE stack, power management
Panel selection (1280×800 RGB888), touch controller (GT911), timing init
4G modem integration, antenna placement, co-existence testing
Wireless coil design, shielding, USB-C output, certification
ABS + aluminum enclosure, RF window, antenna keep-out zone

items.title: Confirm 1280×800 panel supplier and validate AC792N RGB timing at 40MHz
Layout review: DCVDD14/WFVDD14 1.4V DCDC routing per Figure 4.5
Verify plastic RF window in top 20mm of shell (antenna keep-out zone)
Port LVGL v8.x to JL_SDK and validate 30 FPS on 1280×800
Define BLE GATT service spec for Wi-Fi credential exchange
Run co-existence test: 4G Tx vs AC792N WiFi Rx interference
Source ±10ppm 24MHz and 32.768kHz crystals with approved vendor
Qi coil mu-metal shielding validation vs AC792N DCDC inductor

version_history.title: Document Version History
rows: Initial hardware spec based on AC792N unified SoC. Replaced dual-chip (ESP32-S3 + AC6966) with single-chip architecture.

footer.line1: QR114i Salaam Stream Hub — Project Development Bible v1.0
footer.line2: This document is a living reference.

team_react.title: Team: Mobile APP Development
team_react.subtitle: Mobile Application (iOS/Android) & Cloud Sync Server

scopes.heading: 1. Mobile App Features
items: BLE GATT client — scan for QR114i-Service and provision Wi-Fi credentials
Prayer time API integration — sync timetable to device over WiFi
Location services — auto-detect city for correct prayer times
Reciter selection — 15 reciters with preview playback
OTA trigger — initiate firmware updates from app

scopes.heading: 2. Cloud Sync Server
items: REST API endpoint: GET /api/prayer-times?city=&date=
Authentication: JWT-based device registration
OTA hosting: versioned .fw binaries with SHA256 checksum
Analytics: device heartbeat, error logs

deliverables: iOS + Android app (React Native or Flutter)
BLE provisioning flow with error handling

team_pcba.title: Team: PCBA Design
team_pcba.subtitle: AC792N Schematic & 4-Layer PCB Layout

scopes.heading: 1. Power Tree
items: AC792N requires: DVDD33 (3.3V), DVDD12 (1.2V), DCVDD14/WFVDD14 (1.4V DCDC — Figure 4.5)
Battery: 22,000mAh Li-Ion with fuel gauge IC
USB-C PD: Charging input + mobile device charging output
Qi wireless coil: Isolated from DCDC inductors (>30mm or mu-metal shield)

scopes.heading: 2. Audio Routing
items: AC792N DAC output → Class-D amplifier → Speaker
Analog ground plane isolation — no digital traces beneath analog section
Follow Section 4.5 & 4.6 of AC792N datasheet strictly

scopes.heading: 3. Crystal & Clocks
items: 24MHz main crystal: ±10ppm tolerance (Section 2.2)
32.768kHz RTC crystal: ±10ppm tolerance for timekeeping
Placement: within 5mm of AC792N, minimise trace length

deliverables: 4-layer PCB Gerber files with impedance control notes
Layout review report (DCDC, audio, RF zones)

team_firmware.title: Team: Firmware Development
team_firmware.subtitle: JeiLi AC792N — JL_SDK / FreeRTOS

scopes.heading: 1. Display & UI
items: LVGL v8.x ported to JL_SDK — 30+ FPS target on 1280×800 RGB888
Prayer time UI: next prayer countdown, current time, reciter name
Settings screen: language, brightness, volume

scopes.heading: 2. Azan & Audio Logic
items: Timer: 32.768kHz RTC Crystal for ±2min/year accuracy
Wake-up: RTC alarm wakes AC792N from sleep mode
Playback: SD Card → DAC → amplifier. Directory: RECITER_01/azan.mp3

scopes.heading: 3. WiFi & BLE Management
items: WiFi STA Mode: Connect to home router for NTP and prayer time API sync
BLE Server: Advertise QR114i-Service for Mobile App configuration

scopes.heading: 4. Power State Machine
items: Monitor VBAT to detect USB-C power loss
Battery Mode: Reduce CPU clock to 80MHz. Disable EN_4G_PWR. Disable Wireless Charger

deliverables: .fw Update Binary (USB Direct Upgrade via PC Tool)
RF Calibration Report: ±20ppm deviation on 24MHz XTAL

team_tft.title: Team: TFT Screen
team_tft.subtitle: 7" Display & Touch Integration

scopes.heading: 1. Panel Selection
items: Resolution: 1280×800 (RGB888) — validated on AC792N at 30+ FPS
Interface: 40-pin FPC (24-bit RGB, HSYNC, VSYNC, DE, CLK)
Timing: Pixel clock ~40MHz. Front porch 8, back porch 8, sync width 4

scopes.heading: 2. Touch Panel
items: Method: I2C Capacitive Touch Controller (e.g., GT911)
Connection: GPIOs configured as I2C in firmware
Interrupt line: active-low INT connected to AC792N GPIO

deliverables: Panel Timing Initialization Code (Front Porch, Back Porch, Pixel Clock)
LVGL Display Driver for AC792N SDK

team_router.title: Team: Built-In Router (Habolink AF007)
team_router.subtitle: 4G LTE Connectivity
items: Isolation: Router power rail (3.8V) is switched OFF by AC792N when VBUS is absent (Battery Mode)
Antenna: Keep router antenna >15mm away from AC792N's onboard PCB Antenna at all times
Integration: SIM Slot on bottom edge of ABS shell
Co-existence: Must pass 4G Tx vs AC792N WiFi Rx interference test at DVT phase
deliverables: Co-existence Interference Test Report (4G Tx vs AC792N WiFi Rx)

team_charger.title: Team: Built-In Mobile Charger
team_charger.subtitle: Wireless Charging & USB-C Output
items: Qi Wireless Coil: Shielded with mu-metal to prevent interference with AC792N DCDC inductor
Physical separation: >30mm between Qi coil and DCDC inductors, or mu-metal shield required
USB-C Output: 5V/2A mobile device charging port on side of unit
Safety: Overvoltage and thermal protection
deliverables: Magnetic Interference Test Report (Qi coil vs DCDC Inductor)

team_shell.title: Team: Enclosure
team_shell.subtitle: Mechanical Design — ABS + Aluminum

antenna_note.title: PCB Antenna Keep-Out Zone (Critical — Datasheet Section 5)
antenna_note.body: The top 20mm of the ABS Inner Shell MUST be completely plastic. No metal in the antenna keep-out zone.

scopes.heading: RF Window Design (Critical)
items: The AC792N has an onboard PCB Trace Antenna requiring a clear RF window
The 1.8mm Aluminum Frame acts as a Faraday Cage — blocks RF
Design Change: Top 20mm of ABS Inner Shell MUST be completely plastic
Clearance: 8-10mm No-Metal zone around the antenna trace (Figure 5.1)

scopes.heading: Mechanical Requirements
items: Display: 7" panel cutout with 40-pin FPC routing
SIM slot: accessible on bottom edge
USB-C ports: charging input + mobile output on side
Volume/power buttons: side-mounted tactile switches
Speaker grille: front-facing, minimum 10cm² open area

deliverables: RF Window Design Verification (Plastic region in Aluminum frame)
Antenna Zone Mechanical Drawing (8-10mm clearance documented)
